invariant_definition: The triple intrinsic-nature description of *brahman*: *sat* (being), *cit* (consciousness), *ānanda* (bliss). All Vedāntic schools accept *saccidānanda* as a description of *brahman*; they differ on whether the three are merely identical aspects of one undifferentiated reality (Advaita: yes — *saccidānanda* is *svarūpa-lakṣaṇa* of *brahman*, internally undivided) or are real distinguishable qualities (Viśiṣṭādvaita, Dvaita: *brahman* really has *sattva*, *jñāna*, *ānanda* as real *guṇas* among others). The user's position-brief connects *saccidānanda* to the simultaneous static-and-dynamic character of the Real (T1, T9, Auxiliary Integrations).

per_school.definition: *Saccidānanda* is the *svarūpa-lakṣaṇa* (intrinsic-nature description) of *brahman*; the three aspects are not really distinct from *brahman* or from each other but are three pointers at the same undifferentiated reality.
*Brahman* really has *sat*, *cit*, *ānanda* as constitutive features along with infinite other auspicious qualities (*ananta-kalyāṇa-guṇa*).

translator_note: 'Being-Consciousness-Bliss' is the standard gloss. The compound is one of the most-cited descriptions of *brahman* in the tradition. Note: the *ānanda* is not subjective pleasure but a metaphysical aspect of the Real.
